preamble: The voiceless retroflex affricate [ʈʂ] deaffricates and merges with the voiceless alveolar fricative [s]. This change involves the loss of the stop component and the movement of the place of articulation from retroflex to alveolar.

examples.note: Characteristic of the 'zh' > 's' merger in many non-standard Mandarin accents and Southern dialects.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
